item.title: Hey Imgur, long time no meme! Meet Figaro (Fig).
item.description:
Figs first day! Imgur meet Figaro. Aka Fig, Figgy or Figbert. He's a smol lil guy. Brave and sleepy.
Don't be fool by his smol size. He is a menace. We were supposed to pick him up last week but wanted to get the place ready for him. Surprise! I get laid off this week...Again! Woohoo. I made it 10 months this time. *cries*
We brought him home yesterday and at first he was very shy and skeptical but after half an hour and one wand toy later he is feeling right at home. So confident and brave.
We've been eating, playing for six hours, had a tandem pee which was fun, mewing (tini tiny meows), tiny little purrs, using the scratching post, cuddling, biting Moni, biting my hands...biting my feet. We're gonna have to train this lil monster.
I cherished every cuddle with Sylvester. After he passed last year I never thought I'd get lucky enough to get that experience again but here I am writing this with Fig nestled in my neck just like him.
We've had a long day. You'll be seeing us around. Especially since I got laid off hah. If you know anyone who needs a designer let me know: Itsmoni.com in the meantime I'll be updating my portfolio and resume.
Also thank you to the people who reached out, hoping to update you guys on the apartment situation. https://imgur.com/gallery/Uz3Dy6A Cause boy is it somehow worse than the first three updates, funny for you guys a nightmare for me. The cliff notes for now: confirmed the construction crew damaged our ceiling, they (and landlord) wouldn't pay for relocation so was homeless for two weeks during the ice storm (thankfully stayed at my parents), pushed out of our uninhabitable torn out apartment, was forced to move. (*we've spoken with lawyers it's just a long process). Finally got settled into the new place then got laid off on Monday. Fig is helping ease the blow.
item.dc:creator: ItsMoni

item.title: The aptly named “Climate Spiral”. A cool little visualization from NASA.
item.description: I was actually researching Betelgeuse’s instability when I got distracted by NASA’s YouTube sites. I thought this was really creative and interesting, and there’s more to love here… https://m.youtube.com/@nasascientificvisualizatio5842
item.dc:creator: Firehoser

item.title: Dogs are treated better
item.description:
I don't understand. I'm sitting in this room right now, listening to my mother's death rattles. Soon, they say. Fuck. This has been a three-day ritual. We want to be here for her when my mom dies. She is finally succumbing to her Alzheimer's. I've spent two and a half years watching whatever was left of my mom disappear. Her memories of me and her beloved grandchild. She didn't remember who we were. She knew that she loved us. That's something. But the past three days have been either of her clutching at the sky in anxiety or fear or pain, or so drugged up that there can be no thoughts left. And I don't know. Maybe there's some of her left in there.
To imagine some consciousness left in her mind...some comprehension as she struggles on her last breaths of life. If any part of her is still in there, she would be scared out of her mind. At no point in the last year has she really had any idea of what was going on. What's the point of this? Dogs get a simple shot and that's it. There is zero percent chance of her ever coming back. Zero. Soon, they say. edit: Thank you, everyone. I don't have words right now.
item.dc:creator: DogsThatGoMoo

item.title: Images of Note: Have a Fungus
item.description:
Hey City Council, Linux Mint will save you so much money
LOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOONG
I am God's Mistake!
Square
This is a real church. No crack or horrific extremism happening here.
After the events of Frontier day, everyone under the age of 25 now has nightmares about Janeway, making things very awkward for her.
Yeah, just not Confederate Wizards
I remember saying "thank fuck 2019 is over" and then 2020 hit.
I still get carded at 33.
They wouldn't let him up there because that was where the Warner Brothers (and the Warner Sister) live.
The Prime Minister of the UK in the year 3004 is there trying to make people accept their deaths as to not disrupt the economy.
I don't even know where this is from but this is a vibe.
uwu
Yeah that's just me tbh
For Chats: http://discord.abbii.gay For Shitposts: http://tumblr.abbii.gay For srs and maybe shitposts: http://mastodon.abbii.gay

item.title: Let's Talk about Teleportation...
item.description:
First, spoiler warnings for SOMA, The Prestige, Black Mirror, and Fat Farm. I'll be delving into their plot twists and secrets, so if you haven't played SOMA, read Fat Farm, or seen The Prestige or USS Callister, go do that now. Teleportation is one of those magical future technologies that we see in sci-fi media all the time, and other than the occasional glitch for plot, they all work pretty flawlessly. You can go from a spaceship in orbit straight down to a specific point on the surface in no time at all.
Now, I want to quickly point out that Teleportation and Portals/Wormholes are very different. Teleportation is breaking down your physical form, and reassembling it in another location. A Portal or Wormhole is instead ripping a hole through space and transporting you very quickly across a great distance, with some Portals reaching several hundred thousand miles (The Moon - Portal), and certain Wormholes reaching several billion miles. (Alaris - Stargate)
Lots of little things can go wrong, however. There could be the problem of transmission errors. Sometimes when you print a page, the paper slips a little, and the font doesn't quite line up. Imagine your veins and arteries being just slightly misaligned. MIGHT cause some problems for you, and especially if you're zipping back and forth regularly. And don't get me started on the tumors. That little trip to the liquor store before they closed could just cost you your life.
As you can imagine, things can also go HORRIBLY wrong with teleporters. You could have an inexperienced technician (Or god forbid, A.I.), or a solar flare throwing shit off, or maybe the Walmart you want to teleport to had a power outage, and never received you. There have to be redundancies, safeguards, there has to be logical thought to this. But safety can come with unforeseen consequences, and you should prepare for those.
In The Prestige, the main character buys a machine from Tesla that he hopes will transport him so he can best his magician rival with a superior magic act. The machine however doesn't work that way. It clones whatever material is inside it, first cloning his hat multiple times as Tesla tests the machine, and then his assistant's cat. They are alerted to the cloning when the two identical cats are found fighting outside, amid a sea of hats. He still uses the machine for his transported man act, but he can't have copies of himself running around, so he kills himself every time he performs the trick. The man steps into the machine, is duplicated into the distance (Teleported), and then the original falls through a trap door during the flash of light, and lands in a locked box filled with water. The clone than appears behind the crowd, finishing the show and reveling in the applause, all while he also drowns below the stage.
In SOMA, we meet a man in Canada that is signing up for a new kind of brain scan. He heads to the clinic, gets into the machine, and then wakes up at the bottom of the ocean, centuries in the future. As he explores this sunken hell, he learns more about the place, and what is going on. Everyone on the underwater station also had a brain scan like he did, everyone is stored digitally inside the computer system. But then when the systems started failing, the computer tried to save them all by dumping the brain scans into any robot bodies it could find. Including, of course, the template mind in the scanning program, the guy who volunteered to do a scan way back in Canada. He actually got to live out his life just fine, but his scan woke up after the computer dumped his brain into a robot. He had no memories of his life after the scan, because that was AFTER the scan. Throughout the game, you are presented with horrible choices, of putting these inhuman robot people out of their misery, or leaving them there, stuck and alone at the bottom of the ocean. Including, eventually, you. Throughout the game we are introduced the the concept of the Coin Toss. This is similar to what the character in the Prestige had to go through, not knowing if he would be the man in the box, or the man in the Prestige. Well, in SOMA, you need to access a deeper part of the facility, and that requires a hardier robot body, so you have the scan of a woman help you transfer yourself. You wake up in the new body, but can hear your old body asking why it didn't work, just before he goes to sleep. You're then left with a choice, terminate him, or leave him to wake up, down here, unable to proceed in his weak body, alone forever. At the end of the game, you're trying to get all the scans onto a digital space ark where people can live happy lives in the Matrix, basically. You time the launch with your transfer to keep things streamlined, but suddenly the buffer bar slows when transferring you. It hits 100% right as the ship launches, and you celebrate, but then you stop, asking why you aren't on the ark right now. The female scan tries to explain to you again that you ARE on the ark, but YOU aren't. You lost the coin toss. Eventually you piss her off so bad she short circuits and dies, and so you are once again stranded and alone at the bottom of the ocean.
So, now with all that in mind, let's look again at safe Teleportation, and the horrors needed to operate it. Firstly, we want to make sure that we arrive at our destination fully intact before our original form is destroyed. Likely once it starts building you at the destination, your original would be rendered unconscious so as to not have a full blown panic attack that you're about to kill it. You would verify that blood is not spurting from your lungs, and choose to destroy your original. This would likely be watered down to a "Do you feel fine? Y/N" and you just click Yes, not having to think about the murder you just committed. (Or is it suicide?) Obviously we can't have two of you running around, from a Social Security standpoint, as well as a population standpoint. Who lives in your house? You do, but so would your original, and your original's original. Things would get messy and complicated fast. Second, we should have back-ups in case something happens. Say fives years after your first teleport, you discover you have cancer. Wouldn't it be neat if you could have your current brain scanned and copied into your first original teleported form? That way you retain the memories, but you won't currently have your cancer, AND you would be able to start treating it before it becomes a life threatening problem, as you have a five year head-start. Well, now we need to look at just what could go wrong with these safeguards. Security does come with losing freedoms, after all.
So about those digital backups... Let's delve into a little Black Mirror. Robert Daly is a gifted programmer and co-founder of a popular sci-fi MMO game. He doesn't get respect from his coworkers, so he takes his little bits of revenge on digital copies of them, stealing their DNA from the workplace and creating digital sentient copies of them in his own private game where he is the captain of the ship, and able to alter and torture them in any way he wants. He can remove your genitals, he can throw you into space where you suffocate, but don't die, he can bring in DNA of your real-world kid so you can watch THEM be tortured instead of you. And no one in the real world is aware he is doing this to their digital copies.
In Fat Farm, Mr Barth uses a cloning service to lose weight every few years. He eats whatever he wants, gets huge, and then goes and has himself cloned thin. He takes over his own assets and goes back home, only to repeat the cycle, with the agreement that his fat self be allowed to live in lavish comfort. What he finds though, is that his original form, now devoid of resources, is to either be euthanized, or made into their slave. And his new clone has no idea this is happening, at least until he comes back the next time as the new fat version of himself. So, let's look at those safeguards. First being able to have two versions of you at the same time. That is a great way to harvest a slave workforce, isn't it? You went to Walmart, but you also left a fully functioning body behind. Seems an awful waste to just incinerate it. Let's put it to work. So instead of arriving at Walmart, you wake up in a cell, with no options at all. Work, or death. It doesn't really matter to them, they'll get another chance at a slave when you come home from Walmart. Hell, they don't even have to wait for that, they have access to your back-up scan, remember? They can print more of you, expecting to arrive at Walmart, any time they like. Maybe they don't even need you to work, maybe they just need your kidney. Since you're a disposable clone, there's no need for pain relief, or to even kill you afterwards, you'll bleed out eventually. But hey, up in the real world, they managed to find you a matching kidney, isn't that great?
So think about all that the next time you complain about traffic on the way to the store. At least you aren't being sold into slavery just because you wanted some salsa. Thanks for coming to my horrifying TED Talk.
item.dc:creator: Onlyhereforthelaughs

item.title: Sometimes I really think I chose the wrong profession
item.dc:creator: LosPer

item.title: Faux News should lose their FCC license
item.dc:creator: stopfGemBaron

item.title: It's my 6th cakeday, have a favorite
item.description:
Imgur never reminds me, this is my first on-the-day cakeday post, squeaking in under the 30 minute mark because I remembered looking at cake day posts yesterday and was like "oh when's mine again?" You can't even check it on Android get ya shit together imgur
So here's a favorite of mine with my hero Spidey. No joke, actual "i didn't have any role models growing up hero," Spider-man!
So thanks imgur for the good times and the bad, you're fucking weird and I NEED YOU TO CHERISH THAT. CHERISH IT.
Anyway here's unrelated Belgian techno anthem Pump Up The Jam
item.dc:creator: GravyEducation
